ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather data entry */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the weather station where the data was collected */,
  "time" TEXT /* Time of the data collection */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Temperature in degrees Fahrenheit */,
  "dew_point" REAL /* Dew point in degrees Fahrenheit */,
  "humidity" INTEGER /* Humidity percentage */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Wind speed in miles per hour */,
  "wind_gust" REAL /* Wind gust in miles per hour */,
  "pressure" REAL /* Atmospheric pressure in inches of mercury */,
  "precipitation" REAL /* Precipitation in inches */,
  "wind_direction" TEXT /* Wind direction (e.g., NNW, NW) */,
  "condition" TEXT /* Weather condition (e.g., Fair, Rain) */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the data collection */,
  "collection_method" TEXT /* Method used to collect the data (e.g., manual, automated) */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data (e.g., good, bad) */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version of the data */,
  "links" TEXT /* Links to related data or resources */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_wind_direction FOREIGN KEY ("wind_direction") REFERENCES wind_directions ("direction_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_condition FOREIGN KEY ("condition") REFERENCES weather_conditions ("condition_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather station */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the weather station (e.g., City, State, Country) */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the station */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographical latitude of the station */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographical longitude of the station */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the station in feet */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the station was installed */,
  "maintained_by" TEXT /* Organization responsible for maintaining the station */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_conditions" (
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather condition */,
  "condition_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather condition (e.g., Fair, Rain) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the weather condition */,
  "condition_code" TEXT /* Unique code for each weather condition */,
  "condition_category" TEXT /* Category of the weather condition (e.g., precipitation, temperature) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("condition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "wind_directions" (
  "direction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each wind direction */,
  "direction_name" TEXT /* Name of the wind direction (e.g., NNW, NW) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the wind direction */,
  "direction_code" TEXT /* Unique code for each wind direction */,
  "direction_category" TEXT /* Category of the wind direction (e.g., north, south) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("direction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_files" (
  "file_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data file */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather station the file belongs to */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the data file */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the data file in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the file was uploaded */,
  "file_format" TEXT /* Format of the file (e.g., csv, json) */,
  "file_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the file */,
  "file_category" TEXT /* Category of the file (e.g., historical, real-time) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("file_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_files_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., meteorologist, data analyst, admin) */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the user's role */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather data being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the weather data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the weather data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_method" TEXT /* Method used to access the data (e.g., API, web interface) */,
  "access_status" TEXT /* Status of the access (e.g., successful, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES weather_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather station where the alert was generated */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of the alert (e.g., severe storm, flood warning) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message of the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date of the alert */,
  "alert_time" TEXT /* Time of the alert */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., active, resolved) */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., low, high) */,
  "alert_category" TEXT /* Category of the alert (e.g., weather, non-weather) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)
